content.problemStatement:
For a **binary** string TTT, let f(T)=f(T) = f(T)= the number of modes‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† of string TTT.

For example, f(101)=1f(101) = 1f(101)=1, as it has a unique mode 111 while f(01)=2f(01) = 2f(01)=2, as it has 222 modes, both 000 and 111.

Given a **binary** string SSS of length NNN, compute the value of ‚ąĎL=1N‚ąĎR=LNf(S[L,R])\displaystyle{\sum_{L = 1}^{N} \sum_{R = L}^{N} f(S[L,R])}L=1‚ąĎN‚ÄčR=L‚ąĎN‚Äčf(S[L,R]), where S[L,R]S[L,R]S[L,R] denotes the substring SLSL+1‚Ä¶SRS_LS_{L+1}\ldots S_RSL‚ÄčSL+1‚Äč‚Ä¶SR‚Äč.

‚Ä†^\dagger‚Ä† Mode of a string is the character with maximum frequency. If multiple characters have maximum frequency, all of them are said to be modes. For example, string 111111111 has mode 111 while string 101010 has 222 modes, both 000 and 111.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN - the length of the string.
The second line contains a binary string SSS of length NNN.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the required sum.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 10510^5105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111 :

f(0)=1f(0) = 1f(0)=1, as it has only 111 mode which is 000.
f(1)=1f(1) = 1f(1)=1, as it has only 111 mode which is 111.
f(01)=2f(01) = 2f(01)=2, as both 000 and 111 are modes.

The total sum is 444.
Test case 222 :

f(0)=1f(0) = 1f(0)=1, as it has only 111 mode which is 000. The substring 000 has two occurrences in the string.
f(1)=1f(1) = 1f(1)=1, as it has only 111 mode which is 111. The substring 111 has two occurrences in the string.
f(00)=1f(00) = 1f(00)=1, as it has only 111 mode which is 000.
f(01)=2f(01) = 2f(01)=2, as both 000 and 111 are modes.
f(11)=1f(11) = 1f(11)=1, as it has only 111 mode which is 111.
f(001)=1f(001) = 1f(001)=1, as it has only 111 mode which is 000.
f(011)=1f(011) = 1f(011)=1, as it has only 111 mode which is 111.
f(0011)=2f(0011) = 2f(0011)=2, as both 000 and 111 are modes.

The total sum is 121212.
